structure.title: Preface
structure.summary: The preface of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document provides an overview and essential information regarding its content. It introduces key terms such as AUTOSAR, design models, target configuration models, and M1 model-based configuration approaches. The document outlines important relationships between different components, including parameters and references, and specifies constraints for their use. Additionally, it details the change history of the document and its relationship to specific AUTOSAR releases, highlighting additions, changes, and deletions in each release cycle.

structure.title: Introduction
structure.summary: This section introduces the configuration model at the M2 level for the AUTOSAR adaptive platform, focusing on its role in generating target configurations. Key concepts include the distinction between design-level and target-configuration models, with the M2 model serving as a structural foundation for individual functional clusters. The summary highlights that this document provides the necessary framework for creating detailed configuration models tailored to specific functions within the platform. While no tables are explicitly mentioned, the section emphasizes the hierarchical relationship between different levels of modeling (M1 and M2) in the AUTOSAR adaptive platform's architecture.

nodes.title: Document Conventions
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" outlines the conventions used in the document to ensure clarity and consistency in technical communication. It introduces key concepts such as the use of mono-spaced fonts for technical terms, unique numerical constraint IDs for defining constraints on the AUTOSAR meta-model, and class tables that list attributes with specific meanings. The section also describes when and how these constraints should be enforced during different stages of an AUTOSAR workflow to prevent misconfiguration. Additionally, it specifies the format of class tables and the use of verbal forms from a standard template for expressing obligations.

structure.title: Conceptual Aspects
structure.summary: The "Conceptual Aspects" section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" delves into foundational ideas and principles that underpin the adaptive machine configuration system. It introduces key concepts such as dynamic reconfiguration, which allows machines to adjust their operational parameters in response to changing environmental conditions or user needs, and resilience, ensuring that systems can recover from failures without significant downtime. The section also outlines the relationship between these concepts, emphasizing how dynamic reconfiguration enhances overall system resilience by enabling more flexible and responsive operations. While no specific tables are mentioned, the discussion provides a clear framework for understanding the core mechanisms and goals of adaptive machine configuration.

nodes.title: Design and Target Configuration Models
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" explains the distinction between design models and target-configuration models within AUTOSAR. It introduces key concepts such as "target-configuration," which refers to configurations specific to a particular target, like ECU Configuration (EcuC) for an EcuInstance on the classic platform or Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration (APMC) for machines on the adaptive platform. The section clarifies that while the distinction between these models is generally clear, there are elements that combine aspects of both design and configuration, making the boundary sometimes unclear. It also notes a shift from using "deployment" to "target-configuration" to avoid confusion, emphasizing that target-configuration focuses on local configurations specific to targets in AUTOSAR platforms.

nodes.title: Design Model
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on defining design-related model elements within the context of system design. It introduces key concepts such as the System Designer role and the criteria for identifying design-related models based on their contribution to the overall system's external behavior. The summary highlights that these elements, like network baud rates or CAN IDs, are critical as they cannot be altered during target configuration without breaking the system. Additionally, it emphasizes the role of design-related model elements in facilitating communication between different roles involved in the development process.

nodes.title: Target Configuration Models
nodes.summary: This section discusses the target-configuration models in the context of an adaptive platform, focusing on how these models are created and their relationship to design-level models. Key concepts include model elements that are typically local to a compute node and are often generated during integration. The summary also highlights that some target-configuration elements maintain direct relations with design-level elements, while others are derived from the content of design-level elements. Notably, unlike the classic AUTOSAR ECU Configuration model which seeks a standalone configuration containing all necessary information, APMC assumes parts of the design model will be available on the target in a machine-specific format, reducing redundancy and potentially simplifying the configuration process.

nodes.title: Overview
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" provides an overview of the configuration process on the AUTOSAR adaptive platform compared to the classic platform. Key concepts include the M2 and M1 models, where the M2 model defines the structure for serialization into XML schema, while the M1 model is serialized as ARXML files. The section emphasizes that the "value side" (M1) must adhere to the structure defined by the "definition side" (M2), ensuring consistency between parameter definitions and their actual values. These relationships are illustrated through Figures 2.1, 2.2, and 2.3, highlighting the hierarchical nature of model serialization and validation processes in AUTOSAR adaptive platform configuration.

nodes.title: Benefits of using an M1 model-based configuration approach
nodes.summary: This section discusses the advantages of using an M1 model-based configuration approach for the AUTOSAR adaptive Platform compared to a more advanced (M2) level implementation. Key concepts include simpler model structures that facilitate easier translation into vendor-specific configurations, and the ability to define default values, use function-cluster-specific terminology, and specify multiplicities beyond standard ranges. The approach also allows for flexible schema changes without backward compatibility concerns, ensuring adaptability to evolving requirements while maintaining clear separation between design and configuration workflows.

nodes.title: Modeling Strategies for deriving M1 Model Content
nodes.summary: This section discusses the modeling strategies for deriving content in the M1 model of the AUTOSAR adaptive platform. It emphasizes that configuration models often rely on elements from the design model to provide a comprehensive picture. Different approaches are considered for integrating information from both design and configuration levels, highlighting the interdependence between these two aspects of the system. While no specific tables are mentioned, the section underscores the necessity of combining detailed design specifications with configuration details to ensure accurate and complete platform configurations.

nodes.title: Only Design exists
nodes.summary: This section discusses scenarios where the configuration of a functional cluster can be entirely derived from a design model, eliminating the need for an initial configuration model. It introduces the concept that in certain rare cases, only a design model is necessary to define the system's structure and behavior without requiring additional configuration steps. The summary also mentions Figure 2.4, which visually represents this modeling strategy where only a design model exists, highlighting the relationship between design and configuration processes in such exceptional scenarios.

nodes.title: Fully derive Design Model to Configuration Model
nodes.summary: This section discusses a modeling strategy aimed at fully deriving all relevant information from the design model into the configuration model. The key concept here is that once this derivation process is complete, the design model becomes redundant and can be deleted. This approach mirrors the AUTOSAR classic Platform's method for configuring the Com stack but has been criticized for leading to a large and complex configuration model due to potential information duplication. While effective, this strategy requires careful management to avoid excessive complexity in the resulting configuration model.

nodes.title: Partially derive Design Model to Configuration Model
nodes.summary: This section discusses an alternative approach to deriving a configuration model from a design model by partially deriving it and maintaining flat references or instanceRefs into the original design model. Key concepts include the use of AUTOSAR type-prototype pattern, which is not utilized for parts requiring flat references, and the optional nature of this derivation process. The strategy emphasizes the use of instanceRefs to connect the configuration model with the design model, particularly in areas where the type-prototype pattern was applied during meta-model creation. Figures 2.6 and 2.7 illustrate these strategies visually, showing how the configuration model can maintain references back to the design model through either flat references or instanceRefs.

nodes.title: Only Configuration exists
nodes.summary: This section discusses the modeling strategy where no design model exists; instead, all configuration is performed at the M1 level. It highlights a specialized scenario that does not reflect typical practices. The key concept introduced is the exclusive focus on configuration without any underlying design model. This approach is illustrated through Figure 2.8, which provides a visual representation of this modeling strategy. While no specific technical details from tables are mentioned in the provided content, it emphasizes the uniqueness and constraints associated with configurations at the M1 level.

nodes.title: Vendor Specific Functional Cluster Definition
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" discusses the definition and usage of Vendor-Specific Functional Cluster Definitions within the AUTOSAR framework. Key concepts include the distinction between Standardized Functional Cluster Definitions provided by AUTOSAR and Vendor-Specific Functional Cluster Definitions, which can extend these standards with additional elements. The document introduces the `ApmcFunctionalClusterDef` category attribute to differentiate between standardized and vendor-specific definitions. Additionally, it outlines constraints on the use of this attribute, emphasizing that only specific values are reserved and custom values are not currently supported.

structure.title: Overview
structure.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" provides an overview of the configuration elements and modeling strategy for the target-configuration. It introduces two key components: the "definition side," which outlines the structure of the configuration for specific parts, such as a functional cluster; and the "value side," which assigns values to these configurations based on the defined structure. The section highlights that these two aspects are interdependent, with the definition side setting up the framework within which the value side operates. While no tables are explicitly mentioned in this overview, the modeling approach emphasizes a structured and systematic method for configuring adaptive platforms.

nodes.title: Abstract Structure
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" outlines the abstract structure defining the relationship between the "value side" and the "definition side" in the machine configuration model. Key concepts include `ApmcAbstractDefinition` and `ApmcAbstractValue`, which represent the "definition" and "value" sides, respectively. The document specifies that a reference from `ApmcAbstractValue` to `ApmcAbstractDefinition` establishes this relationship, with no 1:1 correspondence between them. Important relationships are detailed in tables 3.1 and 3.2, which list the subclasses of both abstract classes and their attributes, emphasizing that multiple values can reference a single definition. Constraints include the requirement for an `ApmcAbstractValue` to have a valid `definition`.

nodes.title: Definition Side
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document focuses on defining key elements and their relationships within the target-configuration framework for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It introduces two primary classes: `ApmcDefinitionCollection` and `ApmcFunctionalClusterDef`. The `ApmcDefinitionCollection` acts as a central collector, allowing multiple references to be managed through a single entity that points to various `ApmcFunctionalClusterDefs`. Meanwhile, the `ApmcFunctionalClusterDef` class defines the configuration of individual functional clusters. Both classes are marked as "candidate" status and are specific to the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. The table for `ApmcFunctionalClusterDef` details its attributes, including an aggregation with `ApmcContainerDef`, which is tagged with a split key for further segmentation.

nodes.title: Definition Elements
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on defining key elements for modeling classes in the target configuration of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It introduces the abstract class `ApmcDefinitionElement`, which provides essential attributes such as multiplicity (`lowerMultiplicity` and `upperMultiplicity`), creation method (`elementCreation`), and origin (`origin`). The document also outlines constraints on these attributes, ensuring that either `upperMultiplicity` or `upperMultiplicityIsInfinite` must exist for each element. Additionally, it describes the use of an index to maintain order in collections where necessary, which is particularly relevant when the M1 level does not support ordered collections by default.

nodes.title: Grouping of Configuration Elements
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on how configuration elements are grouped within a container structure in the context of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include `ApmcParamConfContainerDef`, which defines containers for parameters and references, and `ApmcContainerElementDef`, representing elements within these containers. The document emphasizes the ability to create nested structures through aggregation and the use of choice-containers to emulate inheritance. Importantly, it describes how these containers can be targets for references that link different nesting levels and highlights the implementation details provided in Tables 3.8, 3.9, and 3.10, which outline the attributes, base classes, and relationships between various container definitions.

nodes.title: Emulation of Inheritance
nodes.summary: This section discusses the emulation of inheritance in the Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration (APMC) through the use of `ApmcChoiceContainerDef`. Key concepts include a choice container that allows for specifying choice semantics at the definition level, enabling a behavior similar to sub-class creation in the M2 model. The relationship between the target-configuration's APMC and the M2 model is illustrated using an example where inheritance-like structures are created via the APMC. Table 3.11 details the attributes of `ApmcChoiceContainerDef`, including its aggregation with other containers, which further elucidates how this meta-class facilitates the emulation of inheritance in the system's configuration.

nodes.title: Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on defining and managing configuration parameters within the context of an adaptive platform. It introduces key concepts such as `ApmcParameterDef`, which is an abstract class serving as the base for various parameter definitions, and specifies that these parameters can only be defined in a `ApmcParamConf-ContainerDef`. The section outlines different subclasses like `ApmcBooleanParamDef` and `ApmcIntegerParamDef`, each tailored to specific data types. Table 3.12 details the attributes of `ApmcParameterDef`, providing a structured overview of its components, while emphasizing that this class is exclusive to the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform.

nodes.title: Reference
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" discusses the concept and implementation of references within the context of target-configuration for the Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the abstract meta-class `ApmcReferenceDef`, which serves as a foundational class for various types of reference definitions, such as foreign references and instance references. The document explains that references can be used to establish semantic links between different parts of the configuration or to associate specific elements with components from the design model. While no attributes are explicitly detailed in the provided table, it is noted that `ApmcReferenceDef` is an abstract class utilized by the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform and has several subclasses, indicating a flexible and modular approach to reference management.

nodes.title: Value Side
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document focuses on the value side of the target configuration, detailing how actual configuration values are provided and must reference corresponding definitions for correct interpretation and validation. Key components include `ApmcFunctionalClusterValue`, `ApmcContainerValue`, and `ApmcContainerElementValue`, each required to have a defined reference to their respective configurations on the definition side. The document also introduces the `ApmcValueCollection` class, which acts as a collector for multiple functional cluster values. Important constraints ensure that references from these value elements to definitions exist, and relevant technical details are provided in tables outlining the attributes and relationships of these classes within the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform framework.

nodes.title: Grouping of Configuration Values
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document discusses how configuration values are grouped and structured within the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the `ApmcContainerValue` meta-class, which serves as a container for aggregating elements, and the abstract `ApmcContainerElementValue` meta-class that defines the elements contained within these containers. The relationship between these classes is detailed in Table 3.16 and Table 3.17, where `ApmcContainerValue` aggregates `ApmcContainerElementValue`, and each element has a corresponding definition (`ApmcContainerElementDef`). These definitions are crucial for linking the "value" side of configuration to its "definition" side in the machine configuration model.

nodes.title: Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" delves into the definition and classification of configuration parameters within the context of the Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the abstract base class `ApmcParameterValue`, which serves as a foundational representation for parameter values on the "value" side of the platform, with its concrete implementations detailed through subclasses such as `ApmcAbstractRestrictedTextualParamValue`, `ApmcNumericalParamValue`, and `ApmcTextualParamValue`. The class is part of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform and inherits from multiple base classes, indicating a complex but structured hierarchy. Relationships are established through aggregation by `ApmcContainerValue.element`, linking parameters to container values. While specific attributes are not detailed in this excerpt, Table 3.18 provides further insight into the structure and relationships of these parameter value types.

nodes.title: Reference
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document discusses the `ApmcReferenceValue` abstract base class, which is crucial for representing references on the "value side" within the Adaptive Platform. Key components include its subclasses such as `ApmcAbstractInstanceReferenceValue`, `ApmcAbstractReferenceValue`, and `ApmcContainerReferenceValue`. The document also notes that `ApmcReferenceValue` is used exclusively by the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, inheriting from multiple base classes including `ARObject` and `Identifiable MultilanguageReferrable`. Relationships are established through aggregation with `ApmcContainerValue.element`, though specific attributes are not detailed in the provided information.

structure.title: Details
structure.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" delves into the intricate details of configuring elements within an adaptive platform. It introduces key concepts such as configuration parameters and their interdependencies, which are crucial for optimizing machine performance. The summary highlights important relationships between different components and any constraints that must be considered during setup. Additionally, it includes relevant technical details from accompanying tables, providing a comprehensive understanding of the configuration processes involved in the adaptive platform.

nodes.title: Definition Side
nodes.summary: The section titled "Definition Side" in the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on providing a comprehensive description of configuration elements that are crucial for defining machine configurations within an adaptive platform. Key concepts and definitions related to these elements, such as configuration parameters, variable settings, and their interdependencies, are introduced. The summary highlights important relationships between different configuration components and any constraints or processes involved in their interaction. Additionally, the section may include relevant technical details from accompanying tables that outline specific parameter values, default settings, and allowable ranges for various configurations.

nodes.title: Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the detailed definition and modeling of parameter definitions within an adaptive platform. It introduces key concepts such as the manifest parameter definition, which is illustrated in Figure 4.1. The section likely outlines important relationships between different parameters and any constraints or processes involved in their configuration. While no specific tables are mentioned, the overview provided sets the stage for understanding how these parameters interact to support the adaptive capabilities of the platform.

nodes.title: Enumeration Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document outlines the definition and default value assignment for enumeration-style parameters in the context of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include `ApmcEnumerationParamDef` and `ApmcEnumerationLiteralDef`, which are used to model enumerations and their specific values, respectively. The section also describes how a default value can be defined for these parameters, with the goal of providing reasonable initial settings until users make informed decisions. Relationships between classes are established through aggregation, such as the role `literal` in `ApmcEnumerationParamDef`. Table 4.1 details attributes and relationships of `ApmcEnumerationParamDef`, while Table 4.2 provides a similar breakdown for `ApmcEnumerationLiteralDef`.

nodes.title: Boolean Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the definition and usage of boolean parameters within the context of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the `ApmcBooleanParamDef` meta-class, which is used to define configuration parameters that carry a boolean value, and its corresponding class `Apmc-NumericalParamValue` for handling these values on the "value side." The section also introduces the `defaultValue` attribute, allowing for the specification of default boolean values. Importantly, it specifies that this meta-class must adhere to certain regulations defined by `[constr_10584]`, and notes that `ApmcBooleanParamDef` is only utilized within the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Table 4.3 provides a detailed breakdown of the attributes associated with `ApmcBooleanParamDef`, including its relationship with other classes like `ARObject`.

nodes.title: Integer Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the definition and usage of integer parameters within the context of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the `ApmcIntegerParamDef` meta-class, which is used to define configuration parameters with integer values, and its corresponding value side class `Apmc-NumericalParamValue`. The section introduces attributes such as `defaultValue`, `min`, and `max`, which allow for setting default values and defining the allowed range of the parameter. These attributes ensure that the parameter's value remains within specified limits, thereby maintaining consistency and correctness in configuration settings.

nodes.title: Float Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document focuses on the `ApmcFloatParamDef` meta-class, which is used to define floating-point configuration parameters in the context of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the associated `ApmcNumericalParamValue` class and attributes such as `defaultValue`, `min`, and `max`. These attributes allow for setting default values and defining valid ranges for the parameter. The document also specifies that this meta-class is specifically used within the AUTOSAR framework, with relevant technical details provided in Table 4.5, which outlines the structure and attributes of `ApmcFloatParamDef`.

nodes.title: Unrestricted String Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document discusses the `ApmcStringParamDef` meta-class, which is used to define configuration parameters that can hold string values with unrestricted content. Key components include attributes such as `defaultValue`, and relationships with other classes like `ApmcTextualParamValue`. The class is specifically utilized by the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform, and its definition includes details on how default values are applied if no specific value is assigned during instantiation. Table 4.6 provides a structured overview of these attributes and their properties.

nodes.title: Restricted String Parameter
nodes.summary: This section discusses the implementation of restricted string parameters in the Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration (APMC) for various use cases. It introduces key meta-classes such as `ApmcAbstractRestrictedStringParamDef` and its subclasses like `ApmcIpV4AddressParamDef`, `ApmcRevisionLabelParamDef`, etc., each defining specific patterns or conventions that the string parameters must follow. These meta-classes are derived from an abstract base class to ensure uniformity in validation, with attributes for default values and regular expressions for content restrictions. The section emphasizes the importance of formal strictness through the use of specific meta-classes and regular expressions, enabling consistent validation at both definition and value sides of configuration parameters.

nodes.title: Reference
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the detailed modeling of references in the definition side, as illustrated in Figure 4.3. Key concepts include three types of references: container references, foreign references (or so-called M2 model element references), and instance references (referred to as instanceRef). The section outlines important relationships between these reference types and their respective sections for detailed descriptions. While no tables are mentioned, the discussion provides foundational information necessary for understanding how different elements within the system interact through various reference mechanisms.

nodes.title: Reference to Container
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the use and semantics of container references within the M1 model for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include `ApmcContainerReferenceDef` and `ApmcChoiceContainerReferenceDef`, which are used to reference containers, with specific attributes like `destination`. The section outlines that only instances of `ApmcContainerDef` can be referenced, and introduces the concept of a choice container reference where multiple targets exist but only one is active at any given time. Tables 4.20 and 4.21 detail these meta-classes, their attributes, and relationships to other elements in the model.

nodes.title: Foreign Reference
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the modeling and semantics of foreign references within the context of the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include the abstract base class `ApmcAbstractForeignReferenceDef`, which defines how to establish a flat reference to model elements outside the target configuration, and its subclasses such as `ApmcUriForeignReferenceDef` and `ApmcUpstreamDocForeignReferenceDef`. The document outlines that these references can be used in different scenarios: `ApmcUriForeignReferenceDef` allows for references where the target may not exist in the same model scope, while `ApmcUpstreamDocForeignReferenceDef` is specifically designed to reference elements from the design domain that initiated the creation of a container. The section also details attributes like `destinationType`, which identifies the class of the reference target, and discusses how these references are aggregated and used within the platform's architecture.

nodes.title: Instance Reference
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document discusses the instance references used in the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. It introduces key meta-classes such as `ApmcAbstractInstanceReferenceDef`, which defines structured references to model elements, and its subclasses like `ApmcInstanceReferenceDef` and `ApmcUriInstanceReferenceDef`. These classes enable referencing design-related model content from a configuration domain. The document also outlines the attributes `destinationContext` and `destinationType`, which restrict reference targets within specific contexts or target types. Additionally, it describes how these references can be used to identify upstream design elements that influenced the creation of certain configurations, distinguishing between structured and flat references based on their scope and semantics.

nodes.title: Value Side
nodes.summary: The section titled "Value Side" in the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on detailing configuration elements that pertain to the value side of the platform. Key concepts introduced include specific parameters and settings designed to optimize resource allocation based on user-defined values and performance metrics. The section outlines important relationships between these configurations and their impact on system efficiency, as well as constraints related to resource limits and compatibility requirements. Additionally, it includes relevant technical details from accompanying tables that provide thresholds and default settings for various configuration options, aiding in the precise tuning of platform operations.

nodes.title: Parameter
nodes.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on the semantic attributes and definitions related to indexable values within a collection. It introduces key concepts such as `ApmcIndexableValue`, which is an abstract meta-class that enables defining model elements carrying an index in the target-configuration, and discusses how these indices are used to represent logical positions with dedicated semantics. The section also explains that gaps can exist in the value range of ordered collections, where ordering is based on numerical sorting rather than the usage frequency of each index value. Tables 4.30 and 4.31 provide further technical details, specifying attributes like `index` as a positive integer used to identify elements within a collection, and elaborating on the relationships between different classes and their attributes.

nodes.title: Unrestricted Textual Parameter Value
nodes.summary: This section of the "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" document discusses the handling of textual parameters in an adaptive platform machine configuration system. It introduces key concepts such as `ApmcEnumerationParamDef` and `ApmcStringParamDef`, explaining that enumeration-style parameters can only accept predefined values, while string parameters allow for arbitrary input. The section emphasizes the importance of using appropriate parameter definitions to ensure correct value assignment. Table 4.32 outlines the structure of `ApmcTextualParamValue`, providing a framework for understanding how these textual parameters are defined and managed within the system.

nodes.title: Restricted Textual Parameter Value
nodes.summary: This section discusses the modeling and implementation of restricted textual parameter values within the Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration (APMC) framework. It introduces key subclasses such as `ApmcAbstractRestrictedTextualParamValue`, which are used to define parameters with specific value restrictions, and their concrete implementations like `ApmcRevisionLabelParamValue` and `ApmcIpV4AddressParamValue`. The section emphasizes that each subclass must reference the corresponding definition class (e.g., `ApmcRevisionLabelParamDef`). Additionally, tables provide detailed attributes for these classes, such as `value`, which holds the actual restricted value. Important constraints are also outlined, ensuring that references in specific roles only point to designated target types.

nodes.title: Reference
nodes.summary: This section of the document "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on defining and explaining various reference meta-classes used in the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform. Key concepts include `ApmcContainerReferenceValue`, which refers to another container within the same model, and several subclasses such as `ApmcAbstractReferenceValue`, `ApmcForeignReferenceValue`, `ApmcUriForeignReferenceValue`, `ApmcUpstreamDocForeignReferenceValue`, `ApmcAbstractInstanceReferenceValue`, `ApmcInstanceReferenceValue`, `ApmcUriInstanceReferenceValue`, and `ApmcUpstreamDocInstanceReferenceValue`. These classes establish references to model elements either within or outside the target-configuration, with constraints on their existence and usage. The relationships between these meta-classes are defined through inheritance from common base classes like `ARObject` and `ApmcAbstractValue`, and they are aggregated by `ApmcContainerValue.element`. Attributes such as `value` provide specific details about the referenced elements, including whether they must exist or serve only as identifying factors.

structure.title: Modeling Use Cases
structure.summary: This section of "Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration.pdf" focuses on detailing specific modeling use cases where various model elements are combined to achieve predefined higher-level semantics according to the AUTOSAR standard. Key concepts include the integration and application of these model elements in practical scenarios, highlighting their interaction and combination for more complex system configurations. The summary outlines important relationships between different components and any constraints or processes necessary for their effective implementation. While no specific tables are mentioned, this section likely provides technical details on how to apply these models in real-world adaptive platform machine configurations, ensuring alignment with AUTOSAR standards.

nodes.title: Functional Cluster interacts with Functional Cluster
nodes.summary: This section discusses the interaction between functional clusters in an Adaptive Platform Machine Configuration (APMC) context. It introduces key terms such as FunctionalClusterEndpointSpecifier and outlines a specific modeling approach for interactions where one cluster requests services from another, using examples like persistency requesting encryption services from crypto functionality. The summary also details constraints and relationships, including how endpoints are matched based on their configuration references and the imposition times when these configurations must be complete. Relevant technical terms and abbreviations are defined in tables, such as ApmcContainerReferenceDef and ApmcParamConfContainerDef, which play crucial roles in establishing these interactions.
